heading: National strategy for quality improvement in health care
label: 280j National strategy for quality improvement in health care

Amendments2010—Subsec. (a)(2)(B)(iii). , inserted “(taking into consideration the limitations set forth in subsections (c) and (d) of section 1182 of the Social Security Act)” after “information”.Pub. L. 111–148, § 10302

Interagency Working Group on Health Care Quality, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–148, title III, § 3012124 Stat. 380Mar. 23, 2010“(a).—In GeneralThe President shall convene a working group to be known as the Interagency Working Group on Health Care Quality (referred to in this section as the ‘Working Group’).“(b).—GoalsThe goals of the Working Group shall be to achieve the following:“(1)Collaboration, cooperation, and consultation between Federal departments and agencies with respect to developing and disseminating strategies, goals, models, and timetables that are consistent with the national priorities identified under section 399HH(a)(2) of the Public Health Service Act [] (as added by section 3011 [of ]).42 U.S.C. 280j(a)(2)Pub. L. 111–148“(2)Avoidance of inefficient duplication of quality improvement efforts and resources, where practicable, and a streamlined process for quality reporting and compliance requirements.“(3)Assess alignment of quality efforts in the public sector with private sector initiatives.“(c)Composition.—“(1).—In generalThe Working Group shall be composed of senior level representatives of—“(A)the Department of Health and Human Services;“(B)the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services;“(C)the National Institutes of Health;“(D)the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention;“(E)the Food and Drug Administration;“(F)the Health Resources and Services Administration;“(G)the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality;“(H)the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology;“(I)the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration;“(J)the Administration for Children and Families;“(K)the Department of Commerce;“(L)the Office of Management and Budget;“(M)the United States Coast Guard;“(N)the Federal Bureau of Prisons;“(O)the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration;“(P)the Federal Trade Commission;“(Q)the Social Security Administration;“(R)the Department of Labor;“(S)the United States Office of Personnel Management;“(T)the Department of Defense;“(U)the Department of Education;“(V)the Department of Veterans Affairs;“(W)the Veterans Health Administration; and“(X)any other Federal agencies and departments with activities relating to improving health care quality and safety, as determined by the President.“(2)Chair and vice-chair.—“(A).—ChairThe Working Group shall be chaired by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.“(B).—Vice chairMembers of the Working Group, other than the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall serve as Vice Chair of the Group on a rotating basis, as determined by the Group.“(d).—Report to CongressNot later than , and annually thereafter, the Working Group shall submit to the relevant Committees of Congress, and make public on an Internet website, a report describing the progress and recommendations of the Working Group in meeting the goals described in subsection (b).”December 31, 2010

children.heading: Establishment of national strategy and priorities
children.label: a Establishment of national strategy and priorities

children.heading: National strategy
children.label: 1 National strategy
children.text: The Secretary, through a transparent collaborative process, shall establish a national strategy to improve the delivery of health care services, patient health outcomes, and population health.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall identify national priorities for improvement in developing the strategy under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: have the greatest potential for improving the health outcomes, efficiency, and patient-centeredness of health care for all populations, including children and vulnerable populations;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify areas in the delivery of health care services that have the potential for rapid improvement in the quality and efficiency of patient care;

children.label: iii
children.text: address gaps in quality, efficiency, comparative effectiveness information (taking into consideration the limitations set forth in subsections (c) and (d) of section 1182 of the Social Security Act [, (d)]), and health outcomes measures and data aggregation techniques;42 U.S.C. 1320e–1(c)

children.label: iv
children.text: improve Federal payment policy to emphasize quality and efficiency;

children.label: v
children.text: enhance the use of health care data to improve quality, efficiency, transparency, and outcomes;

children.label: vi
children.text: address the health care provided to patients with high-cost chronic diseases;

children.label: vii
children.text: improve research and dissemination of strategies and best practices to improve patient safety and reduce medical errors, preventable admissions and readmissions, and health care-associated infections;

children.label: viii
children.text: reduce health disparities across health disparity populations (as defined in section 285t of this title) and geographic areas; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ix
children.text: address other areas as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: C Considerations
children.text: In identifying priorities under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall take into consideration the recommendations submitted by the entity with a contract under section 1890(a) of the Social Security Act [] and other stakeholders.42 U.S.C. 1395aaa(a)

children.heading: Coordination with State agencies
children.label: D Coordination with State agencies
children.text: The Secretary shall collaborate, coordinate, and consult with State agencies responsible for administering the Medicaid program under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] and the Children’s Health Insurance Program under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.] with respect to developing and disseminating strategies, goals, models, and timetables that are consistent with the national priorities identified under subparagraph (A).42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The national strategy shall include a comprehensive strategic plan to achieve the priorities described in subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: Coordination among agencies within the Department, which shall include steps to minimize duplication of efforts and utilization of common quality measures, where available. Such common quality measures shall be measures identified by the Secretary under section 1139A or 1139B of the Social Security Act [, 1320b–9b] or endorsed under section 1890 of such Act [].42 U.S.C. 1320b–9a42 U.S.C. 1395aaa

children.label: B
children.text: Agency-specific strategic plans to achieve national priorities.

children.label: C
children.text: Establishment of annual benchmarks for each relevant agency to achieve national priorities.

children.label: D
children.text: A process for regular reporting by the agencies to the Secretary on the implementation of the strategic plan.

children.label: E
children.text: Strategies to align public and private payers with regard to quality and patient safety efforts.

children.label: F
children.text: Incorporating quality improvement and measurement in the strategic plan for health information technology required by the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 ().Public Law 111–5

children.heading: Periodic update of national strategy
children.label: c Periodic update of national strategy
children.text: The Secretary shall update the national strategy not less than annually. Any such update shall include a review of short- and long-term goals.

children.heading: Submission and availability of national strategy and updates
children.label: d Submission and availability of national strategy and updates

children.heading: Deadline for initial submission of national strategy
children.label: 1 Deadline for initial submission of national strategy
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to the relevant committees of Congress the national strategy described in subsection (a).January 1, 2011

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the relevant committees of Congress an annual update to the strategy described in paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: a review of the short- and long-term goals of the national strategy and any gaps in such strategy;

children.label: ii
children.text: an analysis of the progress, or lack of progress, in meeting such goals and any barriers to such progress;

children.label: iii
children.text: the information reported under section 1139A of the Social Security Act [], consistent with the reporting requirements of such section; and42 U.S.C. 1320b–9a

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of an update required to be submitted on or after , the information reported under section 1139B(b)(4) of the Social Security Act [], consistent with the reporting requirements of such section.January 1, 201442 U.S.C. 1320b–9b(b)(4)

children.heading: Satisfaction of other reporting requirements
children.label: C Satisfaction of other reporting requirements
children.text: Compliance with the requirements of clauses (iii) and (iv) of subparagraph (B) shall satisfy the reporting requirements under sections 1139A(a)(6) and 1139B(b)(4), respectively, of the Social Security Act [, 1320b–9b(b)(4)].42 U.S.C. 1320b–9a(a)(6)

children.heading: Health care quality Internet website
children.label: e Health care quality Internet website

children.text: the national priorities for health care quality improvement established under subsection (a)(2);
the agency-specific strategic plans for health care quality described in subsection (b)(2)(B); and
other information, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
